title: Discovery Service: Config Bundle Generation, Signing, and Versioned Delivery for Sentinels
description: Implement the mechanism by which the Discovery Service produces a versioned, cryptographically-signed configuration bundle for each sentinel. The bundle contains all operational parameters the sentinel needs: discovery endpoints, chain config, trust anchors, revocation parameters (Δ), observability config, and policy defaults. Sentinels must verify the bundle signature before applying any configuration. Rollback to a previous version is administrative-only and audit-logged.
objectives: Every sentinel operates with a deterministic, verifiable configuration traceable to a specific bundle version
Bundle signing with Discovery's issuer key prevents tampering in transit or storage
Controlled rollback with full audit trail — rollback is protected by security-admin role

steps: Step 1 - Bundle Schema Definition: Define the canonical JSON structure for a config bundle. Fields: bundle_version (int, monotonically increasing per sentinel), issued_at (ISO-8601), sentinel_did (str), sentinel_id (UUID), role ('producer'|'consumer'), env ('dev'|'test'|'prod'), service_id (str), discovery (object: { sync_url, credentials_url, heartbeat_url, status_list_base_url }), chain (object: { network, chain_id, rpc_urls: list[str], contract_addresses: { issuer_registry, trust_policy_registry, status_registry, service_registry } }), policy_defaults (object: { max_token_age_seconds, allow_unknown_issuers: false, fail_closed_on_chain_error: true }), revocation (object: { delta_seconds, cache_ttl_seconds, mode: 'strict'|'best-effort' }), observability (object: { otlp_endpoint, log_level, trace_sampling_rate }), issued_by (DID of Discovery issuer), signature_kid (key ID used to sign).

Step 2 - ConfigBundleService: Create services/discovery/src/discovery/services/config_bundle_service.py. Method generate_and_sign(sentinel_id: UUID, session: AsyncSession) -> ConfigBundle: (a) load sentinel record, (b) load current Discovery settings, (c) build bundle dict, (d) canonicalize to UTF-8 JSON (sorted keys, no extra whitespace), (e) compute bundle_hash = SHA-256(canonical_bytes), (f) sign canonical JSON as JWS with Discovery issuer key (kid from SecretStorage), (g) persist to config_bundles table (TASK-022), (h) return signed bundle.

Step 3 - JWS Signing: Use python-jose or PyJWT. Sign the canonical bundle JSON as the JWS payload (not the hash — sign the full content). Detached payload format optional; standard JWS compact serialization acceptable. Include JOSE header: { alg: 'EdDSA'|'ES256', kid: '<key_id>', typ: 'config-bundle+jwt' }. The signed_bundle_jws column in config_bundles stores the full JWS string.

Step 4 - Persistence: config_bundles table (TASK-022). Columns: id (UUID PK), sentinel_id (UUID FK), version (INT NOT NULL), bundle_hash (TEXT NOT NULL), signed_bundle_jws (TEXT NOT NULL), issued_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), is_current (BOOL default true). On new bundle: set is_current=false on all prior bundles for this sentinel_id, then insert with is_current=true. Keep last 10 versions (delete oldest if count > 10). Index on (sentinel_id, is_current) and (sentinel_id, version).

Step 5 - Config Bundle Fetch Endpoint: GET /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/config. Auth: sentinel auth token (bearer token issued at onboarding) OR security-admin. Returns latest config bundle (is_current=true). Response: signed_bundle_jws (Sentinel verifies signature before use), bundle_hash, version, issued_at. Add ETag: bundle_hash. Support If-None-Match: if client sends matching ETag → 304 Not Modified. Sentinel must validate: parse JWS, verify signature with Discovery public key, check issued_at within acceptable age, check sentinel_did matches own DID.

Step 6 - Config Bundle Admin List: GET /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/config/history. Auth: security-admin or operator. Returns last 10 versions: [ { version, bundle_hash, issued_at, is_current } ]. GET /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/config/{version} returns specific version JWS. Used for audit and rollback decisions.

Step 7 - Rollback Endpoint: POST /api/v1/sentinels/{sentinel_id}/config/rollback?to_version=X. Auth: security-admin. Validate X exists in history and is not current version. Re-sign the historical bundle with current issuer key (to prevent stale key usage on rollback) — generate a new signed JWS with incremented version = max_version + 1 but content from version X. Mark previous current as not current. Write audit event: action='config_rollback', summary={ from_version, to_version, sentinel_id }. NOTE: rolling back applies the old config content but with a new version number and fresh signature.

Step 8 - Bundle Invalidation on Sentinel Decommission: When a sentinel is decommissioned (TASK-033), call ConfigBundleService.invalidate(sentinel_id). Mark all bundles is_current=false and insert a terminal bundle with empty/disabled config (policy_defaults.all_requests_denied=true). Prevents a re-activated sentinel from operating with stale config.

Step 9 - Key Rotation in Bundle: When Discovery issuer key rotates, config bundles retain their old JWS (historical record). New bundles generated after rotation use new key + new kid. Sentinels must trust both old and new Discovery public key during a rotation window (configured in bundle as trusted_discovery_kids: list[str]).

Step 10 - Webhook Notification (Optional): If webhook_url is configured per sentinel, POST to webhook_url with { event: 'config_updated', sentinel_id, new_version, bundle_hash } after new bundle generation. Sentinel polls as fallback; webhook is best-effort with one retry.

files.services/discovery/src/discovery/api/v1/: GET config, GET history, POST rollback endpoints
files.services/discovery/src/discovery/reposit: DB operations: insert, get_current, get_history, rollback

fields.name: bundle_hash
fields.comment: SHA-256 hex of canonical bundle JSON

api_endpoints.response_304: Not Modified (if If-None-Match matches current bundle_hash)

error_handling.message: No config bundle has been generated for this sentinel
Config bundle version 99 does not exist for this sentinel
Version 3 is already the current config version

security_constraints: Bundle signed with Discovery issuer key (kid included in JWS header). Sentinel MUST verify before applying.
Rollback is security-admin only — prevents operators from downgrading security policy
All rollback events write to audit_events with from_version, to_version, actor identity
Bundle content never includes secrets — only addresses, URLs, DID references, and policy parameters
Sentinels should validate issued_at is within 24h to reject stale bundles (configurable max_bundle_age)

acceptance_criteria: Generated bundle passes JWS signature verification using Discovery public key
bundle_hash SHA-256 matches the canonical JSON of the bundle content
GET /config with matching ETag returns 304 without body
Rollback creates a new version with old content and fresh signature — version is higher than before rollback
Decommissioned sentinel receives a terminal config bundle (all_requests_denied=true)
Config history endpoint returns last 10 versions with is_current correctly set
